fields.Name: Schema Management
fields.Description: Root authorization for all schema management operations via the Entity Designer Agent. Users or roles must have this authorization (or a child) to invoke any Entity Designer functionality.

fields.Name: Create Entities
fields.Description: Grants the ability to create new database entities (tables) through the Entity Designer Agent. Child authorizations further restrict which schemas are permitted.

fields.Name: Create in UDT Schema
fields.Description: Grants the ability to create new entities in the __mj_UDT schema only. This is the most restricted create permission — recommended for most users.

fields.Name: Create in Custom Schema
fields.Description: Grants the ability to create new entities in any non-protected schema (not __mj, dbo, sys, or INFORMATION_SCHEMA). Intended for advanced users who need to extend application schemas.

fields.Name: Modify Entities
fields.Description: Grants the ability to modify existing database entities through the Entity Designer Agent. Child authorizations restrict the scope of what can be modified.

fields.Name: Modify Own Entities
fields.Description: Grants the ability to modify only entities that the current user originally created via the Entity Designer Agent. Tracks ownership via MJ: Entity Settings (UDT provenance metadata).

fields.Name: Modify Any UDT Entities
fields.Description: Grants the ability to modify any entity in the __mj_UDT schema, regardless of who created it. Intended for admins who need to maintain user-defined tables.

fields.Name: Delete Entities
fields.Description: Reserved for future use — grants the ability to delete (DROP) entities through the Entity Designer Agent. Inactive until delete workflow is implemented in a future phase.
